auditors.noSelection: Select an auditor from the list
pcs.noSelection: Select a PC from the list
guide.hero.title: LPM System Guide
guide.hero.sub: Everything you need to manage clients, sessions, academy students and courses — all in one place. Read through each section below to get up and running quickly.
guide.overview.title: Overview
guide.overview.lead: LPM is organised into several areas — main pages, management tools, and administration — all accessible from the left-hand sidebar. Use the navigation menu to move between them at any time.
guide.overview.dashboardDesc: Your weekly session grid. Track how much auditing time each staff member delivered.
guide.overview.pcsDesc: Manage client profiles, payment history, purchased hours and session records.
guide.overview.academyDesc: Record daily student visits, enrol students in courses, and print weekly attendance sheets.
guide.overview.adminDesc: Manage courses, staff permissions, and session approvals. Admin role required.
guide.overview.tip: Your role (Admin or regular staff) determines which pages you can see. If a page is missing from your menu, contact your administrator.
guide.dashboard.title: Dashboard
guide.dashboard.sub: Weekly session tracking for auditors and CS
guide.dashboard.step1text: Use the Prev / Next buttons at the top to move between weeks. The week always runs Thursday → Wednesday. Today’s week is shown by default.
guide.dashboard.step2text: Each column is a staff member. Each row is a day. The cell shows the total billed session time for that person on that day (h:mm). CS columns appear greyed — they count separately in the grand total.
guide.dashboard.step3text: If you are both a regular and solo auditor, a Solo toggle button appears at the top of your dashboard. Switch to Solo mode to record solo sessions separately from your regular auditing time.
guide.dashboard.step4text: Click the Print button to generate a PDF of the weekly grid, including a staff leaderboard and day-by-day statistics chart.
guide.dashboard.step5text: Below the grid you can manage the list of PCs assigned to you this week. Add or remove PCs from your personal list using the selector at the bottom of the card.
guide.pcs.title: PCs (Clients)
guide.pcs.step1text: Type a name or external ID in the Search... box at the top of the left panel. The list filters instantly.
guide.pcs.step2text: Each PC in the list shows a coloured badge — green means hours remaining, red means over-used. The list is sorted with lowest hours at the top so you can spot who needs a top-up quickly.
guide.pcs.add1text: Press the green New PC button. Fill in the required fields — First name and Last name are mandatory. If a person with the same name already exists an error will appear before saving.
guide.pcs.add2text: Add phone, email, date of birth, sex and origin. These can also be filled in later by clicking Edit on the PC’s detail card.
guide.pcs.pay1text: Select a PC, then click + Add Payment in the Payments card.
guide.pcs.pay2text: Auditing — enter hours bought and amount paid. These hours count toward the remaining hours balance. Course — select the course from the dropdown and enter the amount paid. The PC is automatically enrolled in the Academy course.
guide.pcs.pay3text: Registrar defaults to your own name — change it if someone else processed the payment. Referral is optional: set it if the PC was referred by someone.
guide.pcs.tip: Course payments automatically create an open enrollment in the Academy. You do not need to enrol the student manually.
guide.academy.title: Academy
guide.academy.sub: Daily attendance, courses and weekly reports
guide.academy.visit1text: Click the day pill at the top of the page (Mon, Tue, …). The pill turns green when that day has visits. You cannot add visits to future days.
guide.academy.visit2text: Type in the Search students… box. Matching names appear as chips below. Click a chip to add a visit for that student on the selected day.
guide.academy.visit3text: In the visit list on the right, click the trash icon next to a student’s chip to remove their visit for the selected day.
guide.academy.new1text: Press the New Student button in the top-right of the navigation bar.
guide.academy.new2text: First name, Last name and Course are all required. A person with the same full name cannot be added twice — the system will warn you. Select the Referral and Organization if known.
guide.academy.new3text: Saving the form automatically records a visit for the currently selected day, so you don’t need to add it separately.
guide.academy.course1text: In the weekly student table, click the book icon on any student row. A modal opens showing their active and completed courses.
guide.academy.course2text: Click Finish next to an active course, pick the finish date, then confirm. The course moves to the completed list and the student’s active course column clears.
guide.academy.course3text: Use the Add Course dropdown at the bottom of the modal. Choose the course, set the start date, and click Add.
guide.academy.print1text: The Print button (top-right) generates a PDF for the currently displayed week. The PDF includes the student attendance table, referral & org breakdowns, each student’s active course with visit count, and a per-course summary table.
guide.academy.print2text: The PDF opens or downloads automatically. No printer is needed — you can save it or share the file directly.
guide.academy.tip: The Active Course column shows the course name and the total number of academy visits since enrollment — e.g. Communication (12).
guide.admin.title: Admin
guide.admin.course1text: Go to Admin → Courses. Type the course name in the bottom row of the Courses table and press Add or hit Enter.
guide.admin.course2text: Click the pencil icon to rename a course inline, then save with Enter or the save button. Click the trash icon to delete.
guide.admin.course3text: Below the Courses table, the Enrollments table shows every student enrolled in every course — including who registered them, referral, amount paid, and academy visit count.
guide.admin.perm1text: When a staff member’s PC list is updated, a permission request may appear here. Click Approve to grant access or Reject to deny it.
guide.admin.perm2text: Each auditor card has an Allow All toggle. When on, the auditor can work with any PC. When off, you can add individual PCs to their approved list using the dropdown at the bottom of their card.
guide.tips.title: Tips & Tricks
guide.tips.enterText: In most inline forms (adding a course, editing a name) you can press Enter instead of clicking the Save button.
guide.tips.searchText: The PC list and Academy student list filter as you type — no need to press search or confirm.
guide.tips.redBadgeText: A red remaining-hours badge on a PC means they have used more hours than purchased. Follow up on a new payment.
guide.tips.visitCountText: The number in parentheses next to a course name — e.g. Communication (8) — counts all academy visits since the enrollment start date.
guide.tips.logoutText: Click the logout icon in the top-right header bar. Your session expires automatically after 12 hours of inactivity.
guide.tips.pdfText: Both the Dashboard and the Academy pages have a Print button that generates a polished PDF — great for weekly briefings.
guide.tips.collapseText: In Work Review and Client Purchases, click a section header to collapse or expand it — keeps long pages manageable.
guide.tips.moneyBankText: In Payments, toggle the Money-in-bank switch to show or hide deposited payments. Tinted rows indicate already-deposited amounts.
guide.tips.receiptText: Every purchase includes a signature pad. After saving, you can print a receipt with full line items and the customer's signature.
guide.overview.statisticsDesc: Visual charts and data views summarising key metrics across the system.
guide.overview.managementDesc: Manage auditor profiles, course definitions, and a full member directory.
guide.pcs.purchase1text: Select a PC, then click the Purchase button. A multi-item modal opens where you can add Auditing hours and/or Course enrolments in a single transaction.
guide.pcs.purchase2text: For Auditing items, enter hours bought and amount paid. For Course items, select the course from the dropdown and enter the amount. You can add multiple items of different types.
guide.pcs.purchase3text: Choose a payment method: Cash, Credit Card, Check, or Bank Transfer. This information appears in the admin Payments page for tracking.
guide.pcs.purchase4text: Sign on the signature pad (required) and optionally add notes. The signature is saved with the purchase and appears on the printed receipt.
guide.pcs.purchase5text: After saving, click Print to generate a clean A4 receipt showing all line items, totals, date, and the customer's signature.
guide.statistics.title: Statistics
guide.statistics.step1text: Navigate to Statistics from the sidebar menu. The page displays visual charts summarising key metrics across the system.
guide.statistics.step2text: Charts are interactive — hover over data points for details. Use date range filters where available to focus on a specific period.
guide.statistics.step3text: Below the charts, data tables provide detailed breakdowns you can review or export.
guide.management.title: Management
guide.management.auditor1text: Navigate to Management → Auditors. The page shows all auditor profiles with their current status and grade.
guide.management.auditor2text: Click on an auditor to view or update their profile, including active/inactive status and auditor type (Regular, Solo, or both).
guide.management.course1text: Navigate to Management → Courses. Type the course name in the input row at the bottom of the Courses table and press Add or hit Enter.
guide.management.course2text: Click the pencil icon to rename a course inline, then save with Enter. Click the trash icon to delete a course.
guide.management.course3text: Below the Courses table, the Enrolments table shows every student enrolled in every course — including registrar, referral, amount paid, and academy visit count.
guide.management.member1text: Navigate to Management → Members. A table displays all members with their name, last visited date, and status.
guide.management.member2text: Click column headers to sort by name, last visit date, or status. Use the search box to filter the list instantly.
guide.admin.wr1text: Open Admin → Work Review. Each session row shows the auditor, PC, date, duration, and status. Set the Rate and Salary fields, then click Approve.
guide.admin.wr2text: CS review rows appear alongside sessions. Set the CS Salary field and approve. Each session can have at most one CS review.
guide.admin.wr3text: Click a section header to collapse or expand it. This keeps the page manageable when reviewing many sessions at once.
guide.admin.purch1text: Open Admin → Client Purchases. A filterable table shows every purchase with PC name, date, items, total amount, and approval status.
guide.admin.purch2text: Click Approve to confirm a pending purchase. Use Delete to soft-delete, or Restore to bring back a deleted purchase. Toggle Show Deleted to reveal removed entries.
guide.admin.purch3text: Filter by Pending/Approved status, set a date range, or toggle Include Approved to narrow results. Collapsible Auditing and Course tables group items by type.
guide.admin.purch4text: Rows for purchases whose payment has been deposited in the bank appear with a coloured tint, making it easy to distinguish deposited vs outstanding amounts.
guide.admin.pay1text: Open Admin → Payments. Every payment is listed with its method (Cash, Credit Card, Check, Bank Transfer), amount, date, and associated PC.
guide.admin.pay2text: Payments that are overdue display a visual indicator so you can follow up quickly.
guide.admin.pay3text: Toggle the Money-in-bank switch to mark payments as deposited. Deposited payments can be shown or hidden using the filter at the top of the page. Sortable columns let you organise by any field.
guide.admin.msg1text: Open Admin → Messaging. Compose a message and select which staff members should receive it.
guide.admin.msg2text: The system automatically sends a message to all Admin users whenever a new purchase is created, so no purchase goes unnoticed.
academy.noActiveCourseMsg: is not currently enrolled in any course. Do you still want to add a visit?
pcs.signatureReminder: Don't forget to sign before submitting
purchases.title: Purchase Approval
purchases.subtitle: Review and approve pending purchases
purchases.amountMismatch: Payment total does not match items total
pcs.confirmDeleteMsg: This purchase will be marked as deleted. An admin can restore it later.
pcs.confirmFinishCourseMsg: Are you sure you want to mark "{0}" as finished?
pcs.noSignatureNote: Purchase will be saved without a signature
guide.login.title: Login
guide.login.step1text: Type your username and password on the login page, then click Sign In. If the credentials are wrong, an error message appears below the form.
guide.login.step2text: Click the eye icon inside the password field to toggle visibility — useful for checking what you typed.
guide.settings.title: Settings
guide.settings.step1text: Open Settings from the header gear icon. Enter your current password, then type and confirm a new password (minimum 8 characters). Click Update Password to save.
guide.settings.step2text: In the Profile Picture section, click the upload area and choose an image (JPEG, PNG, GIF or WebP, max 2 MB). The photo appears in the header. Click Remove Photo to delete it.
guide.dashboard.step6text: The dashboard shows three colour-coded tables: Auditor table (blue), CS Solo table (purple) and CS table (grey). Each has its own subtotal row. A combined Grand Total bar at the bottom sums Auditor + CS Solo time.
guide.dashboard.step7text: Click Assign PC, then choose a category: Auditor, CS Solo or CS. PCs with pending reviews are highlighted in green. Select the PC name from the dropdown to add it to your weekly list.
guide.dashboard.step8text: Right-click any cell in the grid to open a context menu. Options include Add Session, Add CS Review, Edit Session, and Remove Column — depending on the cell type.
guide.dashboard.step9text: Toggle the Hide Empty switch at the top to show or hide columns (PCs) that have no sessions for the current week.
guide.dashboard.step10text: Below the tables, a text area lets you write free-form remarks for the current week. Remarks are saved automatically when you click away.
guide.dashboard.step11text: A bar chart at the bottom shows your total weekly hours over the last 20 weeks, making it easy to spot trends in your workload.
guide.pcs.table1text: The left-panel table has five columns: PC Name, Hours Left, Total Time, Visits and Hrs Purchased. Click any column header to sort ascending or descending.
guide.pcs.tabs1text: Shows the latest 50 sessions with date, auditor, duration, admin time, free/billed status and approval status. Draft sessions can be edited inline. Approved sessions are read-only.
guide.pcs.tabs2text: Displays active and finished courses for the selected PC. Active courses show the start date; click Done to mark a course as finished. Finished courses show both start and end dates.
guide.pcs.tabs3text: Edit personal details: first name, last name, phone, email, date of birth, gender, origin and external ID. Changes are saved when you click Save Changes.
guide.pcs.pending1text: Below the detail tabs, any purchases awaiting admin approval appear as amber-coloured cards showing the item summary, date and total amount.
guide.academy.chart1text: A bar chart at the bottom of the page shows weekly visit totals over the last 20 weeks. Hover over any bar to see the exact count.
guide.academy.ref1text: When adding a student, choose from four referral types: Don, Friend, Social or Other. The referral breakdown appears in the printed weekly PDF.
guide.academy.noCourse1title: Adding a visit without a course
guide.academy.noCourse1text: If a student has no active course, a warning dialog appears asking whether to add the visit anyway. Click Add Anyway to proceed or Cancel to stop.
guide.statistics.step4text: A table ranks all staff members by total hours worked this week. Columns show Auditing hours, CS Solo hours and Total. Hover over a name to see an avatar card with their details.
guide.statistics.step5text: Breaks down auditing hours by organization (e.g. Haifa, Riga, Other), giving a quick view of where your clients come from.
guide.statistics.step6text: Four metric cards at the top show: Auditing Time (hours worked today), PCs (clients seen today), Students (academy visits today) and BIS (unique visitors). The Week Totals row shows the same metrics summed for the entire week.
guide.statistics.step7text: A multi-series chart plots Auditing Time, Hours Remaining, Hours Purchased and Sales over the last 20 weeks. Use the week selector at the top to navigate.
guide.management.auditor3text: When you select an auditor, four gradient cards appear: Total Sessions, Hours Worked, Free Sessions and Last Session. These give a quick overview of the auditor's activity.
guide.management.auditor4text: Fill in First Name, Last Name and select a Grade from the dropdown above the auditor list, then click Add. The new auditor appears in the list immediately.
guide.management.auditor5text: In the auditor detail card, choose the Type: Inactive, Regular Only, Solo Only, or Regular + Solo. This controls whether the auditor appears in the dashboard tables and which sessions they can record.
guide.management.member3text: Click the red Deactivate button next to any active member to hide them across the entire system. Click the green Reactivate button to restore a deactivated member.
guide.management.member4text: Click the Add Member button at the top of the page. Fill in First Name (required), Last Name, and optionally an External ID. If a member with the same name exists, you must add an External ID to distinguish them.
guide.management.member5text: Each member row shows coloured badges indicating their roles: PC (client), Academy (student) and Staff. This helps identify at a glance who is active in which part of the system.
guide.admin.perm3text: Each auditor's permission card can be collapsed by clicking its header. This keeps the page tidy when managing many auditors.
guide.tips.settingsText: Change your password and upload a profile picture from the Settings page. Access it via the gear icon in the top header.
guide.tips.contextMenuText: On the Dashboard grid, right-click any cell to access quick actions: add a session, edit, or remove a column.
guide.tips.weekNavText: The Dashboard, Academy and Statistics pages all share the same Thu→Wed week cycle. Use the Prev/Next arrows to browse past weeks.
